title: Petrol price drop loading: Relief ahead for motorists
description: NATIONAL NEWS - South African motorists can...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - South African motorists can breathe a sigh of relief with a drop in petrol and diesel prices from Wednesday. The second price reduction in as many months will be welcomed by South Africans as they navigate a hostile economic climate.


> Prices


The Department of Mineral Resources and Energy (DMRE) has announced the price of 93 octane petrol will decrease by R1.05 and 95 octane petrol will drop by 99 cents per litre from Wednesday, 3 July.

The price of diesel (0.05% sulphur) will be cut by 30 cents per litre, while diesel with 0.005% sulphur will drop by 24 cents per litre.

Meanwhile, illuminating paraffin will cost 18 cents less per litre, while the price of LP gas decreases by 22 cents per kilogram.

Read more on Caxton publication, The Citizen

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
